1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"io"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
11		workguard "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/work"
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/lockmetrics"
14		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
15	)
16	
17	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
18	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
19	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
20	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
21	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
22	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:   "work",
25			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
26			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
27	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
28	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
29	
30	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
31	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
32	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
33	bounds.
34	
35	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
36	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
37	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
38	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
39	
40	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
41	  drained     — no ready beads remain
42	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
43	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
44	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
45	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
46	
47	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
48	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
49	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
50	`,
51			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
52	  ddx work
53	
54	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
55	  ddx work --once
56	
57	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
58	  ddx work --watch
59	
60	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
61	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
62	
63	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
64	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
65	
66	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
67	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
68	
69	  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
70	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
71			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
72			RunE: f.runWork,
73		}
74	
75		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
76		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
77		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
78		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
79		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
80		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
81		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
82		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
83		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
84		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-backend", "", "Attempt backend: worktree, local-clone, docker-clone, or in-tree (default: executions.attempt_backend)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("ignore-cooldown", false, "Ignore retry cooldown across this worker session (requires --reason)")
86		cmd.Flags().String("reason", "", "Operator reason required by --ignore-cooldown")
87		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
88		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
89		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
90		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
91		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
92		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
93		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")

127	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
128	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
129	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
130	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
131	// by this layer.
132	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
133		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
134			return err
135		}
136	
137		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
138		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
139		lockmetrics.SetSink(lockmetrics.FileSink(projectRoot))
140		lockmetrics.SetCapEnforcement(projectRoot, "")
141		f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)
142	
143		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
144		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
145		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
146		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
147		if !asJSON {
148			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
149			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
150				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
151			})
152		}
153	
154		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
155	}

227	func countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) (closed, changed, alreadySatisfied int) {
228		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
229			switch attempt.Status {
230			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
231				closed++
232				changed++
233			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
234				closed++
235				alreadySatisfied++
236			}
237		}
238		if closed == 0 && result.Successes > 0 {
239			closed = result.Successes
240		}
241		return closed, changed, alreadySatisfied
242	}

245		switch result.ExitReason {
246		case "drained":
247			return "drained current execution-ready queue"
248		case "once_complete":
249			return "once complete"
250		case "budget":
251			return "budget reached"
252		case "no_progress":
253			return "no-progress policy stopped work"
254		case "blocked":
255			return "blocked waiting for external action"
256		case "operator_attention":
257			return "operator attention required"
258		case "sigint", "sigterm", "context_cancelled":
259			return "stopped by signal"
260		case "fatal_config":
261			return "fatal configuration error"
262		case "preflight_failed":
263			return "preflight failed"
264		case "resource_exhausted":
265			return "resource exhausted"
266		case "routing_unavailable":
267			return "routing unavailable"
268		}
269		if result.StopCondition != "" {
270			return strings.ToLower(strings.ReplaceAll(result.StopCondition, "_", " "))
271		}
272		return "completed"
273	}

16	// TestDirtyRootPreventsClaimLoop verifies that a non-empty ProjectRootDirtyCheck
17	// result stops the worker with a single operator-attention event and zero claim
18	// calls, instead of repeatedly claiming and failing in a churn loop.
19	func TestDirtyRootPreventsClaimLoop(t *testing.T) {
20		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
21		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
22	
23		var execCalls int32
24		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
25			Store: store,
26			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
27				atomic.AddInt32(&execCalls, 1)
28				t.Fatalf("executor must not be called when project root is dirty: %s", beadID)
29				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
30			}),
31		}
32	
33		var logBuf, eventBuf bytes.Buffer
34		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
35		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
36	
37		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
38			Log:         &logBuf,
39			EventSink:   &eventBuf,
40			Once:        false,
41			ProjectRoot: t.TempDir(),
42			SessionID:   "sess-dirty-root",
43			WorkerID:    "worker-dirty-root",
44			ProjectRootDirtyCheck: func(_ string) []string {
45				return []string{"README.md", "src/main.go"}
46			},
47		})
48		require.NoError(t, err)
49		require.NotNil(t, result)
50	
51		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCalls), "executor must not be called on dirty root")
52		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "claim must not be called on dirty root")
53		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
54	
55		require.NotNil(t, result.OperatorAttention)
56		assert.Equal(t, "dirty_project_root", result.OperatorAttention.Reason)
57		assert.Equal(t, []string{"README.md", "src/main.go"}, result.OperatorAttention.DirtyPaths)
58		assert.Equal(t, "OperatorAttention", result.StopCondition)
59		assert.Equal(t, "operator_attention", result.ExitReason)
60	
61		assert.Contains(t, logBuf.String(), "operator attention")
62		assert.Contains(t, logBuf.String(), "uncommitted tracked changes")
63	
64		// Exactly one loop.operator_attention event with reason=dirty_project_root.
65		var attentionCount int
66		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(eventBuf.String()), "\n") {
67			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
68				continue
69			}
70			var entry map[string]any
71			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
72			if entry["type"] == "loop.operator_attention" {
73				if data, _ := entry["data"].(map[string]any); data["reason"] == "dirty_project_root" {
74					attentionCount++
75				}
76			}
77		}
78		assert.Equal(t, 1, attentionCount, "exactly one dirty_project_root operator_attention event")
79	}

81	// TestDirtyRootCleanRootProceedsNormally verifies that a nil ProjectRootDirtyCheck
82	// result does not stop the worker — the loop proceeds to claim and execute.
83	func TestDirtyRootCleanRootProceedsNormally(t *testing.T) {
84		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
85		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
86	
87		var execCalls int32
88		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
89			Store: store,
90			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
91				atomic.AddInt32(&execCalls, 1)
92				return ExecuteBeadReport{
93					BeadID:    beadID,
94					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
95					SessionID: "sess-clean-root",
96					ResultRev: "abc1234",
97				}, nil
98			}),
99		}
100	
101		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
102		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
103	
104		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
105			Once:        true,
106			ProjectRoot: t.TempDir(),
107			SessionID:   "sess-clean-root",
108			WorkerID:    "worker-clean-root",
109			ProjectRootDirtyCheck: func(_ string) []string {
110				return nil
111			},
112		})
113		require.NoError(t, err)
114		require.NotNil(t, result)
115	
116		assert.Nil(t, result.OperatorAttention, "clean root must not trigger operator attention")
117		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "clean root must claim bead")
118		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCalls), "executor must run on clean root")
119	
120		got, err := inner.Get(candidate.ID)
121		require.NoError(t, err)
122		assert.Equal(t, "closed", got.Status)
123	}

28	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
29	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
30	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, powerClass bounds, etc.) live on
31	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
32	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
33		Log             io.Writer
34		CleanupLog      io.Writer
35		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
36		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
37		CleanupInterval time.Duration
38		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
39		EventSink       io.Writer
40		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
41		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
42		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
43		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
44		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
45		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
46		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
47		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
48		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
49		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
50		// ProseEvidenceHook runs after a successful (landed) attempt and before
51		// CloseWithEvidence so docs-changing attempts attach advisory prose-check
52		// evidence to the bead's event history. Errors are logged and ignored;
53		// prose findings never block closure.
54		ProseEvidenceHook ProseEvidenceHook
55		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
56		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
57		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
58		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
59		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
60		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
61		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
62		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
63		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
64		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
65		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
66		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
67		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
68		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
69		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
70		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
71		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
72		// PreClaimTimeout bounds the pre-claim readiness hooks. Zero means use the
73		// binary default so a hanging readiness check cannot park the worker
74		// forever.
75		PreClaimTimeout time.Duration
76		// PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold is the number of consecutive pre-claim warn
77		// fingerprints across distinct bead IDs that trips the operator-attention
78		// guard. Zero means use DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold.
79		PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold int
80		// RouteResolutionTimeout bounds the routing preflight (RoutePreflight) and
81		// the resolveRoute viability check so a hung resolver cannot wedge the
82		// single-threaded worker for hours while a lease is held (ddx-8f2e0ebf).
83		// Zero means use DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout (60s).
84		RouteResolutionTimeout time.Duration
85		// ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold is the number of consecutive wedges
86		// (route_resolution_timeout / progress_watchdog) on the same bead that
87		// trips the consecutive-wedge guard: the worker stops re-claiming the bead
88		// and parks it for operator attention. Zero means use
89		// DefaultConsecutiveWedgeThreshold (2) (ddx-9714eaac).
90		ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold int
91		// ClaimSuccessRateWindow and ClaimSuccessRateThreshold control the rolling
92		// claim-throughput warning emitted by ddx work. A full window whose claim
93		// success rate is at or below the configured threshold triggers a single
94		// operator-visible warning until a later successful claim clears the warned
95		// state.
96		ClaimSuccessRateWindow    int
97		ClaimSuccessRateThreshold float64
98		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
99		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
100		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed. The companion
101		// report is still appended for operator visibility, but the stop itself is
102		// classified through work.StopConditionBudget.
103		BudgetStop func() (work.StopDecision, ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
104		// BinaryRefreshCheck, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next
105		// bead. A true result means the caller has started a replacement worker
106		// with equivalent arguments and this loop should exit before claiming work.
107		// Errors are logged and fail open so update-probing never blocks the queue.
108		BinaryRefreshCheck func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)
109		// ProjectRootDirtyCheck, when non-nil, is called at the top of each loop
110		// iteration before any bead is claimed. A non-empty return value means the
111		// canonical project root has uncommitted tracked non-.ddx changes. The
112		// worker emits a single loop.operator_attention event and stops instead of
113		// proceeding to claim — preventing a churn loop that repeatedly claims then
114		// fails workspace preparation because the root is dirty.
115		ProjectRootDirtyCheck func(projectRoot string) []string
116		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
117		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
118		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
119		Mode         executeloop.Mode
120		IdleInterval time.Duration
121		Once         bool
122		PollInterval time.Duration
123		NoReview     bool
124		// PostMergeReview enables the legacy post-land/pre-close review state
125		// machine for callers that explicitly opt into that manual flow. The
126		// default production close path remains owned by candidate-cycle review.
127		PostMergeReview        bool
128		LabelFilter            string
129		IgnoreCooldown         bool
130		CooldownOverrideReason string
131		SessionID              string
132		WorkerID               string
133		ProjectRoot            string
134		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
135		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
136		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
137		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
138		TargetBeadID string
139		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
140		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
141		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
142		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
143		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
144		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
145		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
146	
147		// FinalizeDurableAudit, when non-nil, is called once per finalized attempt
148		// after the loop has finished writing the attempt's durable tracker state.
149		// Errors are terminal for autonomous work: the worker stops so DDx-managed
150		// audit outputs are not left dirty while more beads are claimed.
151		FinalizeDurableAudit func(report ExecuteBeadReport) error
152		// PostLadderExhaustionHook, when non-nil, is called when a bead's
153		// consecutive_ladder_exhaustions counter reaches the auto-recovery
154		// threshold (>= 2). A nil hook or Attempted=false result falls through to
155		// the existing loop path unchanged.
156		PostLadderExhaustionHook PostLadderExhaustionHook
157	}
158	
159	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
160		mode := r.Mode
161		if mode == "" {
162			if r.Once {
163				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
164			} else {
165				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
166			}
167		}
168		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
169		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
170			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
171		}
172		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
173			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
174		}
175		return mode, idleInterval
176	}
177	
178	// DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout bounds routing preflight and the resolveRoute
179	// viability check when ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.RouteResolutionTimeout is unset.
180	// A hung resolver previously wedged the single-threaded worker for hours while
181	// holding a lease (ddx-8f2e0ebf); 60s is short enough to release promptly and
182	// long enough not to false-trip a slow-but-healthy resolver.
183	const DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout = 60 * time.Second
184	
185	// effectiveRouteResolutionTimeout returns the configured route-resolution
186	// timeout, falling back to DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout when unset.
187	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) effectiveRouteResolutionTimeout() time.Duration {
188		if r.RouteResolutionTimeout > 0 {
189			return r.RouteResolutionTimeout
190		}
191		return DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout
192	}
193	
194	// DefaultConsecutiveWedgeThreshold is the number of consecutive wedges on the
195	// same bead that trips the consecutive-wedge guard when
196	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold is unset. A single transient
197	// wedge (count 1) stays below the default so the bead remains re-claimable; two
198	// consecutive wedges sideline it for operator attention (ddx-9714eaac).
199	const DefaultConsecutiveWedgeThreshold = 2

40	func parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command, treatPassthroughAsOpaque bool) (executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec, executeloop.DispatchOptions, error) {
41		projectRoot, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
42		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
43		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
44		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
45		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
46		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
47		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
48		attemptBackend, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-backend")
49		ignoreCooldown, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("ignore-cooldown")
50		cooldownReason, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("reason")
51		once, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("once")
52		watch, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("watch")
53		rawIdleInterval, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("idle-interval")
54		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
55		dispatchJSON := ""
56		if asJSON {
57			dispatchJSON = "true"
58		}
59		local, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local")
60		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
61		noReviewAck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
62		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
63		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
64		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
65		maxBeadCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-bead-cost")
66		maxRecoveryCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-recovery-cost")
67		preClaimTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("preclaim-timeout")
68		routeResolutionTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("route-resolution-timeout")
69		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
70		rateLimitMaxWait, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("rate-limit-max-wait")
71		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
72		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
73	
74		if noReview && !noReviewAck {
75			return executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec{}, executeloop.DispatchOptions{}, fmt.Errorf("--no-review requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing (break-glass acknowledgement)")
76		}
77		cooldownReason = strings.TrimSpace(cooldownReason)
78		if ignoreCooldown && cooldownReason == "" {
79			return executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec{}, executeloop.DispatchOptions{}, fmt.Errorf("--ignore-cooldown requires --reason \"<text>\"")
80		}
81		if once && watch {
82			return executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec{}, executeloop.DispatchOptions{}, fmt.Errorf("--once and --watch are mutually exclusive")
83		}
84		if !watch && cmd.Flags().Changed("idle-interval") {
85			return executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec{}, executeloop.DispatchOptions{}, fmt.Errorf("--idle-interval only applies in watch mode; pass --watch")
86		}
87	
88		mode := executeloop.ModeDrain
89		var idleInterval executeloop.Duration
90		if once {
91			mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
92		} else if watch {
93			mode = executeloop.ModeWatch
94			idleInterval = executeloop.Duration{Duration: rawIdleInterval}
95		}
96	
97		spec := executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec{
98			ProjectRoot:            projectRoot,
99			Harness:                harness,
100			Model:                  model,
101			Profile:                profile,
102			Provider:               provider,
103			Effort:                 effort,
104			AttemptBackend:         attemptBackend,
105			Mode:                   mode,
106			IdleInterval:           idleInterval,
107			NoReview:               noReview,
108			ReviewHarness:          reviewHarness,
109			ReviewModel:            reviewModel,
110			IgnoreCooldown:         ignoreCooldown,
111			CooldownOverrideReason: cooldownReason,
112			OpaquePassthrough:      treatPassthroughAsOpaque,
113			MaxCostUSD:             maxCostUSD,
114			MaxBeadCostUSD:         maxBeadCostUSD,
115			MaxRecoveryCostUSD:     maxRecoveryCostUSD,
116			PreClaimTimeout:        executeloop.Duration{Duration: preClaimTimeout},
117			RouteResolutionTimeout: executeloop.Duration{Duration: routeResolutionTimeout},
118			RequestTimeout:         executeloop.Duration{Duration: requestTimeout},
119			RateLimitMaxWait:       executeloop.Duration{Duration: rateLimitMaxWait},
120			MinPower:               minPower,
121			MaxPower:               maxPower,
122			FromRev:                fromRev,
123		}
124		spec.ApplyDefaults()
125		if err := spec.Validate(); err != nil {
126			return executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec{}, executeloop.DispatchOptions{}, err
127		}
128	
129		return spec, executeloop.DispatchOptions{Local: local, JSON: dispatchJSON}, nil
130	}

12	// AttemptIntegrityPreserveReason is the canonical landing Reason written when
13	// DDx rejects an otherwise-clean attempt because it violated the execute-bead
14	// integrity contract (post-commit mutation, empty pre-commit gate evidence, or
15	// uncommitted changes after the implementation commit). ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus
16	// and classifyLandingFailureMode match on this exact string to route the
17	// attempt to preserved_needs_review + FailureModeAttemptIntegrity rather than
18	// letting it land as success. The specific human explanation lives in the
19	// result's Error field; this constant is the stable machine marker.
20	const AttemptIntegrityPreserveReason = "execute-bead integrity violation"
21	
22	// CommitEvent is one HEAD-moving entry parsed from a worktree's reflog. Action
23	// is the leading verb of the reflog message ("commit", "commit (amend)",
